learning.concept: Programming is the act of communicating a set of instructions to a computer.
learning.explanation: Programming is like giving directions to a robot. You can only use certain commands that the robot understands. If the robot goes the wrong way, you have to find and fix the bug in your instructions.

analogy.title: Teaching a Robot
analogy.body: Imagine a robot that only knows MOVE, LEFT, RIGHT. If you want it to reach a goal, you have to think step-by-step to get it there. Programming is the bridge between human thinking and robot action.

practice.q: If you tell a robot GO but there is a wall, what happens?
Can you use any word like 'Go' if the robot only understands 'MOVE'?
practice.a: No, you must use specific commands.
It is a skill that takes practice!
